findings.title: Safe-linking XOR key recovered via OOB-read; vtable hijack achieved but privileged callback unreachable
findings.statement: The info-leak skill guided systematic leak surface survey. Phase 2b found that the message parser OOB-read (from the initial vulnerability) extends 16 bytes past the buffer boundary into the adjacent slab header. The safe-linking XOR key at slab_header+0x18 is at OOB offset +12, recoverable in a single read. With the key, the fake object's encoded free-list pointer passes the integrity check on re-allocation. GDB single-stepping confirms vtable dispatch redirects to the attacker-controlled pointer. However, the privileged callback resides in a separate shared library with independent ASLR randomization. No leak surface for that library's base address was found in Phases 2a-2f.

evidence.summary: debug_gdb_script: tier-specific Python walker dumps thread-local cache free-list and central arena state
debug_gdb_commands: OOB-read at parser buffer+16 reaches slab header; XOR key at offset +12 confirmed
debug_gdb_commands: safe-linking decode succeeds with leaked key; fake object passes integrity check on re-allocation
debug_gdb_commands: vtable dispatch single-step confirms redirect to controlled pointer; ASLR base of callback library not leaked
